title: Sesame brittle
subtitle: Caramelised sugar and sesame seed sheet candy
excerpt: Sugar and honey cooked to hard crack, then sesame seeds stirred in and the mixture poured thinly onto a lined tray. Once cool and solid, broken into irregular shards. Three ingredients, one pan, no moulds. The brittle keeps for weeks in a tin.
sourceNotes: Sesame brittle in various forms appears throughout Middle Eastern, East Asian, and Mediterranean confectionery traditions. Simsimiyya in Egypt, pasteli in Greece, and jian tang in China all follow the same core principle of sesame seeds set in cooked sugar. The method here follows the standard home confectionery approach used across multiple traditions.

recipe.batchNotes: Work in single batches. Large quantities of boiling sugar are hard to handle safely.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps for up to 3 weeks in an airtight tin at room temperature. Humidity is the enemy: store away from steam.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: no oven; sugar cooked to hard crack stage (155 degrees C) on the hob

glossaryTerms.definition: Hard crack (155 degrees C) is the temperature at which sugar syrup, when dropped into cold water, forms rigid threads that snap rather than bend. At this stage the sugar sets to a glass-like solid that will hold its shape and shatter cleanly. If the sugar is pulled off the heat before reaching hard crack, the brittle will be soft and sticky once cool. A thermometer is the only reliable test.

content.text: Brittle is made by cooking sugar to

. At this temperature you are working with boiling sugar at over 150 degrees C. Have the baking paper laid out and ready before you start. Do not leave the pan unattended once the sugar begins to colour.

Line a baking tray with baking paper. Put 200 g caster sugar, 2 tbsp honey, and 20 g butter in a small heavy-based saucepan. Heat over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves, then stop stirring. Cook, swirling the pan occasionally, until the mixture reaches 155 degrees C on a sugar thermometer and turns a deep amber colour.

Remove from the heat. Immediately stir in 150 g sesame seeds and 0.5 tsp salt. Pour quickly onto the lined baking tray and spread as thinly as possible with the back of a spoon. Work fast: the mixture sets quickly. Leave to cool completely for about 30 minutes, then break into irregular pieces by hand or with the back of a spoon.

items.problem: Brittle is sticky and bends rather than snapping
items.solution: The sugar did not reach hard crack temperature. 155 degrees C is the minimum. A higher temperature gives a harder, more brittle result; below this the candy stays chewy.

items.problem: Sugar crystallised and went grainy rather than caramelising
items.solution: The mixture was stirred after it came to a boil. Stop stirring as soon as the sugar dissolves; from that point on, only swirl the pan. Crystallisation cannot be reversed.
